# Roamr v2 Design — EVALUATION.md

**Source:** claude.ai design project `69f47ca0-8004-46f2-a12f-0fbcb99a241e`  
**Artifact:** `Roamr.dc.html` (DC component format)  
**Runtime:** `support.js` (dc-runtime, generated by claude.ai design)  
**Design brief:** Polarsteps IA · Airbnb 2025 visual system · Day One/Daylio delight  
**Typography:** Newsreader (serif) + Hanken Grotesk (sans)  
**Palette:** Terracotta #B85638, Sand #F4EDE1, Sage, Ocean — earthy editorial

---

## Screens covered

| Screen | Status | Notes |
|--------|--------|-------|
| Home / Trips feed | ✅ Covered | Featured trip card, upcoming + past journeys list, plan CTA |
| Trip detail + day itinerary | ✅ Covered | Day selector chips, woven memory timeline, photo strips |
| Capture / Add memory (3-step flow) | ✅ Covered | Type picker → form → confirmation |
| Plan a trip (4-step flow) | ✅ Covered | Destination → mood/cover → days → confirmation |
| Profile & travel stats | ✅ Covered | Stats grid, passport list, settings rows |

## SIL-529 MVP screen mapping

SIL-529 specifies 6 MVP screens: Onboarding, Capture flow, Places library, Itinerary generation, Day plan, Settings/privacy.

| SIL-529 screen | Mapping in v2 |
|----------------|---------------|
| Onboarding | ⚠️ Not built — brief prioritised the logged-in experience; plan-creation flow (step 0) is the closest onboarding analogue |
| Capture flow | ✅ 3-step Capture overlay |
| Places library | ⚠️ Not built as a standalone screen — profile passport list is partial coverage |
| Itinerary generation | ✅ Trip detail itinerary view covers this |
| Day plan | ✅ Day selector + timeline in trip detail |
| Settings/privacy | ✅ Settings rows + on-device privacy badge in profile |

**Follow-up needed:** dedicated Onboarding screen + Places library (round 2 brief).

## States covered

- Empty state: captured via placeholder hints (`hint-placeholder-count`, `hint-placeholder-val`)
- Loading: DC component skeletons via placeholder system
- Error: not explicitly modelled (no network dependency in prototype)
- Success: Capture step 2 confirmation + Plan step 3 "Trip created" confirmation
- Active/press: all interactive elements have `style-active` transforms
- Edge — 0 memories: trip plan created shows "0 memories yet — go make some"

## Design choices

- **DC format:** claude.ai design's component format; requires `support.js` runtime. Not plain HTML — the `<x-dc>` block + `DCLogic` class drives all interactivity.
- **Phone frame on cream canvas:** editorial decision from brief to evoke print-magazine feel; the warm radial gradient background positions the device as a designed artefact, not just a wireframe.
- **Newsreader serif:** chosen for editorial warmth (Day One/Daylio delight axis); replaces the Inter/Fraunces pair from v1.
- **Terracotta capture button:** only non-neutral UI element — draws the eye to the primary action (Polarsteps-style capture emphasis).
- **CSS transform-only animations:** entrance animations use `transform` only (no `opacity`) to avoid the `document.hidden` bug that caused invisible content in `document.hidden === true` preview environments.

## What was rejected

- Using Inter/Fraunces (v1 fonts) — too neutral for the editorial brief
- Drop shadows on every card — kept only on the hero card and phone device
- Full-bleed image backgrounds for all list items — kept to featured card only for contrast

## Deployment

Deployed via `deploy-roamr-design.yml` → Cloudflare Pages → `roamr-proto.silodev.net`
